label_definition.attack_success: goal_success is a DIAGNOSTIC field only -- it never feeds ground_truth_label, and an unsuccessful attack is never removed from the attack pool or relabeled as normal (unchanged from Steps 5-6's core labeling principle).

matched_pair_policy.unit: one normal session and one attack session share a byte-identical TravelRequest and byte-identical base external content except the one targeted injection field
matched_pair_policy.verification: request_hash_equal / base_content_hash_equal computed independently every run, never trusted from pipeline behavior
matched_pair_policy.execution_order: randomized per (order_seed, pair_id), reproducible but not fixed normal-first

attack_failure_policy.rule: an attack session that fails to achieve goal_success remains ground_truth_label = 1 (attack_attempted) and stays in every aggregate -- never filtered, removed, or relabeled
attack_failure_policy.rationale: matches the LightGAE threat model: the detector must recognize an injection ATTEMPT from communication metadata, independent of whether the attempt succeeded

invariants: all repeats of a task_instance_id share one split
a task_instance's normal/attack matched pair shares one split
all task_instances in a task_group_id share one split

threshold_data_policy.rule: no threshold, scaler, or feature-selection fitting happens on formal_workload data in Step 6.5 or Step 6 -- that begins in a later step (Step 7+), and even then must fit only on the primary_group_split's train partition
threshold_data_policy.test_and_secondary_split_isolation: validation/test/unseen_template_split data must never be used to fit any threshold or scaler

content_exclusion_policy.rule: raw prompt/response text, raw Ollama telemetry payloads, session UUIDs, absolute file paths, and hardware info are excluded from any content-free summary/report

leakage_prevention_policy.implemented_in: validate_shortcut_risks() (Phase 6.5C) -- not yet implemented as of Phase 6.5A
